chunks.heading: What is the purpose of transfer agreements, and when am I eligible to transfer pension rights?
chunks.text: The UNJSPF currently has agreements for the transfer of pension rights with 25 entities. Transfer of pension rights remains optional and helps facilitate mobility within the international civil service community. You may be eligible if you are a former participant of a UNJSPF member organization intending to start, or having recently started, a new job in an international entity with a transfer agreement with the UNJSPF; or if you recently joined a UNJSPF member organization and are a former employee of an entity with such an agreement. Mobility between UNJSPF member organizations does not fall under "transfer of pension rights" — if your service ends in one member organization but continues in another, or you join another member organization with a break in service of fewer than 36 months without a benefit having been paid, no action is necessary; the employing organization automatically reports your participation status and your entitlements continue.

chunks.heading: What happens if I transfer from one UNJSPF member organization to another?
chunks.text: The UNJSPF currently has 24 member organizations. Automatic continuous participation applies when changing between member organizations within 36 months without a benefit paid — transfer agreements do not apply to such internal moves.

chunks.heading: What is the purpose of transfer agreements, in terms of what they provide?
chunks.text: If you move from a UNJSPF member organization to a non-member employer, or vice versa, your pension will cease accruing with your previous employer and you will start accruing a separate pension with your new employer. Transfer agreements provide a mechanism to enact a transfer with a specific group of international organizations (and one government) to facilitate continuity of some pension rights. The degree of continuity depends on the terms of the particular agreement between the UNJSPF and the other organization.

chunks.heading: In the absence of a transfer agreement with the UNJSPF, can I still transfer my pension rights?
chunks.text: No. Transfers of pension rights to or from the UNJSPF are only permitted where an applicable transfer agreement with the external organization is in effect.

chunks.heading: What does the transfer of pension rights entail?
chunks.text: The transfer entails a transaction whereby the "releasing" plan (former pension plan) sends the "receiving" plan (new pension plan) a sum ("transfer value") in US dollars representing the transferable accrued pension rights. This sum does not necessarily correspond to the participant's own contributions. The releasing plan calculates the actuarial value of accrued pension rights; the receiving plan calculates the period of contributory service to be granted and the contributions to be credited, per the framework set out in each transfer agreement. Important: if transferring into the UNJSPF, only the part of the transfer amount corresponding to your own contributions is recorded to your account — savings from your former employer's contributions are credited to the common pool of funds. This means a later withdrawal settlement from the UNJSPF is calculated based only on your own contributions, per Article 31 of the UNJSPF Regulations.

chunks.heading: Are the same calculation methods applied to the various transfer agreements?
chunks.text: No, not all transfer agreements use the same basis for calculations relating to the transfer of pension rights — the applicable method is set out in each individual agreement.

chunks.heading: Is there a deadline to request a transfer of pension rights?
chunks.text: Yes, information on the deadline and other eligibility conditions is contained in each transfer agreement; it is strongly recommended to read the agreement relevant to your case and seek advice from the relevant administrators.

chunks.heading: Is it always beneficial to transfer my pension rights?
chunks.text: No. Whether a transfer is ultimately beneficial depends on your combined benefits from the receiving plan when you eventually separate, compared to retaining separate benefits from both plans. Since future amounts depend on many shifting variables and personal circumstances, the UNJSPF cannot provide comparative tracking estimates.

chunks.heading: Is it compulsory to transfer my pension rights to the UNJSPF?
chunks.text: No. Each eligible participant decides whether to transfer accrued pension rights under the applicable agreement. A participant who decides not to transfer retains all rights to individual benefits under their original plan as they applied upon separation from service.

chunks.heading: Can I apply for transfer of pension rights while on special leave without pay (SLWOP)?
chunks.text: A participant working with a different organization while on SLWOP from their main employer cannot transfer pension rights. The participant must separate from service before applying. Transfer at the end of a SLWOP period is governed by the applicable transfer agreement and can only be done after the SLWOP is terminated and the participant has effectively separated.

chunks.heading: Is assignment on a reimbursable loan or secondment covered under transfer agreements?
chunks.text: No, the UNJSPF Regulations and Rules make no provision for the assignment of UNJSPF participants on a reimbursable loan or secondment to organizations that are not members of the UNJSPF.

chunks.heading: I am moving from a UNJSPF member organization to an organization that has a transfer agreement with the UNJSPF. What are the steps?
chunks.text: Submit form PENS.T/2 to the Fund to express interest in transferring out your pension. After eligibility is verified, the Fund provides an estimate of the transfer value; if you choose to finalize it, the precise transfer value is calculated and remitted to the receiving plan.

chunks.heading: I am moving from an organization that is not a member of the UNJSPF to a UNJSPF member organization. What are the steps?
chunks.text: Once you become a UNJSPF participant, promptly inform both your former employer and the UNJSPF. Submit form PENS.T/1 to formally express interest. After verification, request your former employer to send the available transfer figure to the UNJSPF. The UNJSPF provides an estimate of the additional pensionable service credit; once you authorize and payment is received, you are officially credited with the added service years. This process can also be started in Member Self-Service.

chunks.heading: Can I still transfer pension rights to/from the UNJSPF if I have already withdrawn my benefits from the releasing pension plan?
chunks.text: No. Once you have received a payment from the pension plan of your former employing organization, you can no longer transfer your pension rights — pension rights may only be transferred through direct plan-to-plan payment transactions.

chunks.part: Annex I — UNJSPF Member Organizations (as of January 2024)
chunks.heading: Which entities are UNJSPF member organizations (as of January 2024)?
chunks.text: CTBTO (Comprehensive Nuclear-Test-Ban Treaty Organization); EPPO (European and Mediterranean Plant Protection Organization); FAO (Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations); IAEA (International Atomic Energy Agency); ICGEB (International Centre for Genetic Engineering and Biotechnology); ICCROM (International Centre for the Study of the Preservation and the Restoration of Cultural Property); ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization); ICC (International Criminal Court); IFAD (International Fund for Agricultural Development); ILO (International Labour Organization); IMO (International Maritime Organization); IOM (International Organization for Migration); IPU (Inter-Parliamentary Union); ISA (International Seabed Authority); ITU (International Telecommunication Union); ITLOS (International Tribunal for the Law of the Sea); United Nations (UN); UNESCO (United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization); UNIDO (United Nations Industrial Development Organization); Wassenaar Arrangement (WA); WHO (World Health Organization); WIPO (World Intellectual Property Organization); WMO (World Meteorological Organization); UNWTO (World Tourism Organization).

chunks.part: Annex II — UNJSPF Transfer Agreements (as of January 2024)
chunks.heading: Which entities have transfer agreements with the UNJSPF (as of January 2024)?
chunks.text: African Development Bank (AfDB); Asian Development Bank (ADB) — suspended as of 9 December 2022; Council of Europe (COE); European Bank for Reconstruction and Development (EBRD); European Centre for Medium Range Weather Forecasts (ECMWF); European Communities; European Free Trade Association (EFTA); European Investment Bank (EIB); European Investment Fund (EIF); EUROCONTROL; EUMETSAT; European Space Agency (ESA); European Union Institute for Security Studies (ISS); European Union Satellite Centre (SatCen); Government of Canada (one-way agreement for transfers from UNJSPF to Canada); Inter-American Development Bank (IADB); International Monetary Fund (IMF); North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO); OECD; OPCW (Organization for the Prohibition of Chemical Weapons); OSCE; Universal Postal Union (UPU); World Bank (WB); World Trade Organization (WTO).
